comprehension_gate.emitted_by: EA-9 (Stage 5 Executor Agent, instance 9; locally first EA of Stage 5 chain)
comprehension_gate.preamble: Iter-1 comprehension gate response per init pointer §required_first_actions Step 3 + §comprehension_gate_template. Pre-Flight Step 0 (rollback-readiness verification per n1(n)) and Step 1 (state.pause_fleet on devplatform per fleet-pause SOP) and Step 2 (V0 pickup verification) all executed; outputs captured in §B below. After this gate, EA-9 STOPS and awaits Guide-#9 PROCEED. No execution beyond Pre-Flight Steps 0-2 until PROCEED.

section_a.name: Stage 5 Objective Restatement
section_a.bullet: **Stage 5 (Cleanup, MEDIUM risk, single-session continuous-block) finalizes BlarAI's reduction to its product-only surface.** Stages 1-4 stood up devplatform and operationally cut over the platform fleet; Stage 5 now removes BlarAI's redundant platform machinery so devplatform is the sole authoritative platform layer. End-state: BlarAI keeps only product code (USE-CASE-001 + USE-CASE-004 + openvino_contrib_agent + BlarAI-internal docs/governance/) plus project-scoped coordination state under `BlarAI/.platform/`.

**Item 5.1: Delete 9 platform tool dirs/files from `BlarAI/tools/`** — `vikunja/`, `vikunja_mcp/`, `fleet_ops/`, `fleet_observability/`, `autonomy_budget/`, `gate_stale_cleaner/`, `scheduled-tasks/`, `_vikunja_client.py`, `_project_context.py`. PRESERVE `tools/openvino_contrib_agent/` (BlarAI-only) and `tools/tests/` (BlarAI-specific). Auto-resolves `BlarAI/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json` dead artifact per w21+dn31+Ticket KK.

**Item 5.0.5 (precondition for 5.1): Audit archive** — capture `*.log`, `*.jsonl`, `*.json.bak`, `state.json*`, `*.history` plus n1(a) extension (`lifts.json`, `escalation_seen.json`) from 6 about-to-be-deleted dirs into `BlarAI/.platform/audit_archive/pre_stage5_.zip` BEFORE 5.1 deletion. Forensic preservation; never run 5.1 first (per dn32 + ack6).

**Item 5.3: Move 4 BlarAI coordination artifacts into `BlarAI/.platform/`** — `sprints/`, `scheduled/`, `docs/active_tasks.yaml`, `docs/sprints/` — wrapped in try/catch with `$applied` rollback list per n1(m) atomicity. Live devplatform fleet reads from `BlarAI/.platform/` post-cutover; the move preserves project state under a clean namespace.

**Items 5.5 + 5.5.v2: Selective doctrine deletion from BlarAI/docs/.** Delete the 7 wake_templates (6/6 fully migrated at Stage 3.2.v2) wholesale. Delete 31+3 platform-doctrine `.md`/`.xml`/`CLAUDE_*`/`CO_LEAD_*`/`DEC*`/`DOMAIN*`/`CONTINUATION_PROMPT` files per n1(d) extension (3 .md peers added: `DOMAIN8_CLARIFICATIONS_v1.md`, `DOMAIN9_KICKOFF_PROMPT.md`, `domain8_spike_findings.md` plus forward-compat `DEC*.md`). **CLASS A LOAD-BEARING per n1(e): SELECTIVELY delete only 4-of-17 governance docs (`STYLE.md`, `fleet-hygiene.md`, `merge-policy.md`, `parallel-sprints.md`); PRESERVE 13 BlarAI-internal architectural docs per A14 disposition** (`README.md`, `circuit-breaker.md`, `configuration-management.md`, `context-spotlighting.md`, `deployment-verification.md`, `error-recovery.md`, `gpu-runtime.md`, `ipc-protocol.md`, `observability.md`, `pgov-validation.md`, `rule-engine.md`, `session-state.md`, `streaming-output.md`). Wholesale `Remove-Item -Recurse -Force docs\governance` is NEVER allowed; would cause irreversible data loss.

**Item 5.4: Devplatform-side audit for stale `BlarAI/tools/` references** — n1(c) aug1 expanded scope (8 globs vs dispatch's 3): `fleet_ops/*.py`, `fleet_observability/*.py`, `autonomy_budget/*.py`, `scheduled-tasks/*.ps1`, `scheduled-tasks/*.xml`, `vikunja_mcp/**/*.py`, `gate_stale_cleaner/*.py`, `docs/scheduled/wake_templates/*.md`. Classify every live-code hit as preserve target-project / preserve historical / DRIFT-requiring-rewrite. Findings flow to Item 5.10 HALT-gate review matrix [1] per n1(k) aug2.

**Items 5.6 + 5.7 + 5.8.v2: BlarAI-side housekeeping** — `pyproject.toml` testpaths REMOVE 6 platform-tool entries + PRESERVE 9 BlarAI runtime entries per n1(g) explicit enumeration; `.gitignore` add `.claude/scheduled_tasks.lock` + `.platform/audit_archive/*.zip` + `.platform/audit_archive/pre_stage5_*/` per n1(h)+w20; `pytest shared/ services/ launcher/ --tb=short -q` baseline expected ~981 passed, 22 skipped per n1(i) supersede-by-5.8.v2.

**Item 5.9: F.2 30-min mini-soak** (REPLACES original `BlarAI_daily_digest` probe — DISABLED per Phase A + wrong-name format). Observe 8 ACTIVE tasks (Wake SDO/Co-Lead/EA Code, Sprint Auditor, Agents Cadence Monitor, Credentials Rotation Reminder, Escalation Watchdog, Gate Stale Cleaner) per their cadence over 30 min real-time post-Item-5.7 commit. ACCEPTANCE: all firing tasks LastTaskResult=0; non-firing tasks NOT failure (Credentials Rotation Reminder daily, Gate Stale Cleaner hourly may not fire in window). Wake-* WORK-GATE skip + NO-WORK-EXIT (NO claude.exe child processes per fleet-paused state).

**Items 5.10 + 5.11 + 5.12 + close unpause: Sealing Stage 5.** Item 5.10 commits on `chore/platform-extraction` (NEVER directly on main; per R10/w10/ack8) and HALTs with the 4-row pre-merge review matrix per n1(k) aug2: [1] 5.4 audit (8 globs PASS/FAIL), [2] 5.8.v2 baseline (~981/22), [3] 5.9 mini-soak (firing-task LastTaskResult=0; non-firing NOT failure), [4] DIFF SUMMARY. LA explicitly approves before merge. Item 5.11 tags both repos (`post-platform-extract` BlarAI, `platform-extracted` devplatform) with n1(l) precondition guard (warn if BlarAI not on `main` post-merge). Item 5.12 STATUS append per Stage 3/4 closure pattern, hash-verified pre-vs-post per d9/A17/EX-4. Stage 5 close LAST action: `state.resume_fleet` on devplatform + `chore(ops): unpause fleet -- Stage 5 cleanup done` commit (per fleet-pause SOP §3 last-action discipline).

**Defensive posture throughout**: fleet stays paused for the duration of Stage 5; rollback escape route is Level-2 (`git reset --hard pre-platform-extract`) per RECOVERY.md — Pre-Flight Step 0 verified the parachute is intact (5/5 PASS). EA-9 does NOT touch BlarAI's state.json directly (dead artifact; auto-resolved at 5.1); does NOT pop or apply any wake_launcher auto-stash (per dn10/w8/A20); does NOT edit BlarAI's CLAUDE.md / .github/copilot-instructions.md / AGENTS.md (per dn3); does NOT autonomously execute Step-0 recovery on failure (LA decisions per ack13/n1(n)). Triple-subagent verification (S1/S2/S3) at Stage 5 close per d6/w14/ack11 with Guide-#9 cross-verifying subagent claims on disk per dn14/dn20/dn24/A18.

section_b.name: Pre-Flight Step 0 + Step 1 + V0 Pickup Outputs

pre_flight_step_0_output.verbatim_powershell_output:
=== Stage 5 Rollback Readiness Pre-Flight ===
[1] PASS: BlarAI tag 'pre-platform-extract' exists [2] PASS: Bundle exists at C:\Users\mrbla\backups\BlarAI_pre_extract.bundle (425.9 MB) [3] PASS: OOG zip exists at C:\Users\mrbla\backups\blarai_oog_20260424_171721.zip (57.6 MB) [4] Computing bundle SHA256 (10-20s)... [4] PASS: Bundle SHA256 matches Stage 0 manifest [5] PASS: devplatform Phase C fix at be22a15 found in git log be22a15 fix(stage4-remediation): wake_launcher reads devplatform state.json + logs to devplatform
=== ALL 5 CHECKS PASS - Rollback parachute is intact. === Authorized to proceed to Step 1 (state.pause_fleet) per Pre-Flight.
pre_flight_step_0_output.interpretation: All 5 critical rollback-readiness checks PASS. Rollback target (`pre-platform-extract` tag), bundle (425.9 MB), OOG zip (57.6 MB), bundle SHA256 (matches `C051CCFB...65D7707` Stage 0 manifest), and devplatform Phase C wake_launcher fix at `be22a15` all confirmed on disk. EA-9 authorized to proceed to Step 1 per n1(n) success path.

pre_flight_step_1_output.action_a: Invoked from Push-Location `C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform`: `state.pause_fleet('Stage 5 cleanup', updated_by='ea_code_blarai_helper', path='C:/Users/mrbla/devplatform/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json')` Returned successfully.
pre_flight_step_1_output.action_b: `git -C C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform add tools/autonomy_budget/state.json` `git -C C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform commit -m "chore(ops): pause fleet -- Stage 5 cleanup"` Result: `[main 1a552d9] chore(ops): pause fleet -- Stage 5 cleanup` — 1 file changed, 4 insertions(+), 4 deletions(-).
pre_flight_step_1_output.verification: paused=True reason=Stage 5 cleanup updated_by=ea_code_blarai_helper updated_utc=2026-04-30T04:27:50Z schema_version=1

field.name: BlarAI HEAD + recent log
field.actual: ff39a41 (init-pointer commit; 1 commit ahead of 176922c) — no Step-1 pause commit on BlarAI side per canonical §step_1 (devplatform-only)
field.log: ff39a41 docs(platform_separation): Stage 5 EA-9 init pointer -- 130-atom ack chain (116 inherited + 14 g9-ea9_n1) + Pre-Flight Step 0 rollback-readiness fold-in + LA pre-prep checklist + Ticket RR fleet-status.ps1 fold-in (Stage 6.7.5 backlog 25 -> 26) 176922c docs(platform_separation): Stage 5.0.v2 audit chain commit + EX-1 wording fix 9e92b26 docs(platform_separation): Guide-#8 rollback-readiness dispatch for EA-9 Pre-Flight Step 0
field.drift_classification: Init pointer's V0 expected text was authored before its own commit landed; metadata.head_at_initiation correctly says "init-pointer-commit (this commit; spawn-time HEAD; recover actual hash via git log --oneline -1)". The V0_pickup_block's expected text "176922c + Step 1 pause commit" is a doc-defect on two counts: (1) the spawn-time HEAD is the init-pointer commit ff39a41, not 176922c; (2) §step_1 canonical instruction commits only on devplatform, not BlarAI. Document and proceed per Operating Rule 5b(a).

field.name: BlarAI working tree
field.expected_per_init_pointer: clean post-Step-1-commit (only the Step 1 pause commit changed; no untracked except .claude/scheduled_tasks.lock)
field.drift_classification: The §step_1 canonical command commits only on devplatform; per §identity hard invariant "EA-9 does NOT touch BlarAI's state.json directly", the M state on BlarAI's state.json (BUG-5 emergency-pause snapshot from Phase B) stays UNCHANGED until Stage 5.1 deletes the parent directory and auto-resolves it (per w21/dn31/Ticket KK). The V0 expected "clean post-Step-1-commit" presumed BlarAI commit advance which does not occur per the canonical §step_1. Document and proceed per Operating Rule 5b(a). Also note: `git -C BlarAI diff --stat` confirms the M state is a 4-line modification (paused-state snapshot) which Stage 5.1 deletion will clean up.

field.name: devplatform HEAD + recent log
field.log: 1a552d9 chore(ops): pause fleet -- Stage 5 cleanup 45aec52 chore(ops): unpause fleet -- guide-8 EX-1 fix + audit chain commit done 0fb794d chore(ops): pause fleet -- guide-8 EX-1 fix + audit chain commit

field.name: devplatform state.json
field.actual: paused=True, reason=Stage 5 cleanup, updated_by=ea_code_blarai_helper, updated_utc=2026-04-30T04:27:50Z, schema_version=1

field.name: BlarAI state.json (path-exists)
field.expected: True (file exists; will be deleted at Stage 5.1)
field.actual: True; diff vs HEAD = 4 insertions/3 deletions (BUG-5 emergency-pause snapshot, dead artifact)

field.name: BlarAI git stash list
field.expected: 2+ wake_launcher auto-stashes from 2026-04-29 wake firings; do NOT pop or apply

field.name: 13/8 task fleet split (Phase A 2026-04-29)
field.expected: 13 total at \BlarAI\; 8 ACTIVE (Wake SDO/Co-Lead/EA Code, Sprint Auditor, Agents Cadence Monitor, Credentials Rotation Reminder, Escalation Watchdog, Gate Stale Cleaner) + 5 DISABLED (Welcome Back Poll, Daily Digest, Weekly Summary, Dashboard Maintainer, Toast Watchdog)
field.actual: 13 total — 8 Ready: Agents Cadence Monitor, Credentials Rotation Reminder, Escalation Watchdog, Gate Stale Cleaner, Sprint Auditor, Wake Co-Lead Architect, Wake EA Code, Wake SDO; 5 Disabled: Daily Digest, Dashboard Maintainer, Toast Watchdog, Weekly Summary, Welcome Back Poll

field.name: MCP configs point to devplatform
field.expected: all 3 MCP configs (Claude Desktop + .vscode/mcp.json + .mcp.json) point to devplatform per Stage 4.4-4.6 cutover
field.actual: DEFERRED — informational only; Stage 5 doesn't modify these. EA-9 will rely on existing Stage 4 cutover state.

field.name: pytest baseline (BlarAI)
field.expected: ~981 passed, 22 skipped on `pytest shared/ services/ launcher/`
field.actual: DEFERRED to Item 5.8.v2 execution (read-only Pre-Flight does not run pytest)

field.name: Vikunja running
field.actual: DEFERRED — informational only; Stage 5 does not query Vikunja

v0_drift_summary.interpretation: Two doc-defects in init pointer's `` expected text both stem from the same root cause: V0 expected was authored against an idealized model where Step 1 pause commits on BOTH repos, but the canonical §step_1 instruction (consistent with §identity hard-invariant "EA-9 does NOT touch BlarAI's state.json directly") commits only on devplatform. BlarAI's M state.json + spawn-time HEAD ≠ 176922c are both consistent with this canonical interpretation. Per Operating Rule 5b(a): document and proceed. EA-9 requests Guide-#9 confirmation in PROCEED that this Operating-Rule-5b(a) classification is correct; if Guide-#9 instead intends BlarAI side to also receive a pause commit (capturing the BUG-5 dead-artifact M state), please direct in PROCEED message. For now, the BlarAI M state is pre-existing (pre-EA-9 spawn) and Stage 5.1 will auto-resolve via parent-directory deletion.

section_c.name: Cumulative-Ack Chain Restatement (VERBATIM)
section_c.intro: Total cumulative-ack chain at EA-9 spawn: **130 binding atoms** (116 inherited + 14 new under g9-ea9_n1). Restatement below preserves IDs + brief verbatim descriptions per init pointer §cumulative_ack_chain_restatement; full source-text remains authoritative in cited verdict files. Section ends with full verbatim restatement of g9-ea9_n1(a)-(n) per the verdict at commit 176922c (Guide-#9-authored bindings).

inherited_chain.ack: Stage 3 Iter-1 verdict APPROVED ack. Stage 3 EA = globally EA-7; A12 records EA-1-vs-EA-7 naming defect; verbatim restate per no-amend doctrine.
Stage 3.1.v2 escalation_watchdog filename + selective tools/+docs/ scope locked: 9 directories + initial runbooks fleet subset.

Stage 3.2.v2 selective governance copy 4-of-17 (STYLE.md, fleet-hygiene.md, merge-policy.md, parallel-sprints.md) + 4 hardening tickets bound + Stage 3.3 expansion authorization for Claude/CO_LEAD/Domain governance pack. **Anchor for n1(e) LOAD-BEARING — the 13 NOT migrated stay at BlarAI per A14.**

Stage 3.0.v2 audit checkpoint, 8 sub-clauses (a)-(h): n13(a) pyproject.toml dependency derivation (httpx, pyyaml, mcp, jsonschema; pytest+pytest-asyncio extras) n13(b) execution_ordering 9-step plan + 5 stop_conditions n13(c) MCP / VS Code config copy 3 sub-targets (per-file top-level keys preserved) n13(d) daily_digest --help non-destructive smoke (exit 0; live-load deferred to Stage 4) n13(e) .gitignore augment (logs/, escalation_seen.json{,.tmp}, devplatform.egg-info/) n13(f) F-N11 promotion tools/tests/ Class D → Class C n13(g) .copy_manifest_v2.yaml schema_version=1 (10 v1 + 21 v2 entries; 195 v2_files) n13(h) forward + inverse drift verification (PASS; A13 F3 relocation accepted)

Stage 3.10 close VERIFIED, 4 sub-clauses (a)-(d): n14(a) single-Stage-3-commit invariant (95884a0; 189 files; +39592/-2; parent 3894221) n14(b) pre-stage state.json SHA256 71FC1A2F…D5D19 UNCHANGED n14(c) BlarAI-side read-only invariant maintained (sole exception: Item 3.10 STATUS append) n14(d) stop_condition status at close: (i)(ii)(iii)(iv) NOT triggered; (v) deferred to Stage 4 live-load smoke

Stage 4 preflight audit + LA decisions, 16 sub-clauses (a)-(p): n1(a) Item 4.0 EA-context preamble + self-termination preflight gate (CC-1) [SUPERSEDED-BY-g8-ea8_n2(b) per supplement 2] n1(b) Item 4.0.6 absolute path on state.json gate command n1(c) Item 4.1 LA-DECIDED 6-step DB location verification + config absolutization (DB stays at %LOCALAPPDATA%; CC-2/CC-3) n1(d) Item 4.2 post-startup verification triad (REST project-count + DB sha256 stability) n1(e) Item 4.3 VIKUNJA_TOKEN LA pre-flight checklist (Parts 0/1/2/3) + REST user/pass auth fallback (CC-7) n1(f) Item 4.4 enumerate all 3 Claude Desktop BlarAI-coupled fields (command + PYTHONPATH + VIKUNJA_PASS) n1(g) Items 4.5+4.6 explicit absolute-path + PYTHONPATH rewrites; VIKUNJA_PASS template (F-1 fix) n1(h) Item 4.7 shape-aware arg-injection + S4U elevation guard for Agents Cadence Monitor (rr5; CC-4) [Step 4 REFINED-BY-g8-ea8_n2(c) per supplement 2] n1(i) Item 4.7.5 forward-slash sibling patterns + preserve target-project refs (CC-6 partial) n1(j) Item 4.7.7 -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue + tighten match regex n1(k) Item 4.9 widen gate to Arguments/WorkingDirectory; rewrite start_vikunja_hidden.vbs (CC-6 main) n1(l) Item 4.11 split EA-8 scriptable smoke (4.11a) vs LA manual smoke (4.11b deferred) n1(m) Item 4.12 gitignore augmentation + explicit allow-list staging (CC-8); explicit staging of devplatform config.yml edit n1(n) Item 4.13.5 two-step reset+resume on devplatform state.json path (CC-5) n1(o) Item 4.4 Claude Desktop trustedFolders expansion for devplatform (per supplement 1) n1(p) Item 4.4 Claude Desktop filesystem MCP expansion for devplatform (per supplement 1)

Stage 4 workflow simplification, 6 sub-clauses (a)-(f): n2(a) Item 4.0 NARROWED kill (vikunja_mcp-only; preserve LA prep-2) n2(b) EA-8 surface = VS Code Copilot Opus 4.7 agent mode native (supersedes n1(a)) n2(c) Item 4.7 S4U paste-relay carve-out (Agents Cadence Monitor only; one paste-relay total) (refines n1(h) Step 4) n2(d) LA prep-3 reframing (elevated PowerShell on-demand for Item 4.7 only) n2(e) surface_specific_directives_for_ea_8 update (VS Code Copilot agent mode canonical) n2(f) iter-1 gate emit update (post-supplement-2)

ack.source: STATUS.md Stage 4 Closure Footnotes §1 + Stage 4 EA-8 disposition files

inherited_chain.ack: Stage 4 execution-time spec refinements, 12 sub-clauses (a)-(l): n3(a-d) regex robustness + venv-stub filter + Pattern B' refinement n3(e) Item 4.7.7 + 4.9 .lnk all-3-fields rewrite + null-safe field access n3(f) 8 NEW Stage 4 work items (4.0.5, 4.0.6, 4.6.5, 4.7.5, 4.7.6, 4.7.7, 4.8.v2, 4.13.5) n3(g) vikunja_project_ids.yaml regex schema fix n3(h) non-idempotency hardening discipline n3(i) Item 4.7.7 sub-check (a) match refinement (deferred) n3(j) Item 4.8 LastTaskResult fleet_paused exit-4 acceptance (later root-caused at A19) n3(k) Item 4.8 elevation paste-relay carve-out (n2(c) precedent extended for 3 Enabled=false tasks) n3(l) Item 4.13 STATUS.md duplication surgical truncate (root cause AMBIGUOUS per A17)
inherited_chain.total: 116 binding atoms (per STATUS.md Stage 4 Closure Footnotes §1: "115 binding atoms total" + n3(l) increment = 116). Cumulative count includes all g7/g8 sub-clauses with their internal sub-letters.

new_g9_ea9_n1.preamble: g9-ea9_n1 is the first ack emitted by Guide-#9 (reviewing EA-9). It binds 14 sub-clauses (a-n) covering all spec corrections surfaced by the Stage 5.0.v2 dual-track audit (Option A subagent + Option B+ inline reconciliation; sub-clauses a-m) PLUS the Pre-Flight Step 0 rollback-readiness verification from Guide-#8 dispatch at 9e92b26 (sub-clause n; added per LA directive 2026-04-29 in lieu of a 4th audit pass). EA-9 restates VERBATIM with all 14 sub-clauses. Sub-clause class summary: A=1 (LOAD-BEARING n1(e)), B=0, C=8, D=5; total = 14. The Class A sub-clause (e) is irreversible-operation-grade and converts a wholesale `Remove-Item -Recurse -Force` to a selective 4-of-17 delete preserving 13 BlarAI-internal architectural governance docs per A14 disposition. Sub-clause (n) is the Pre-Flight Step 0 rollback-readiness verification — 5 critical checks (~30s runtime); HARD STOP on any failure; LA decision required for recovery; runs BEFORE state.pause_fleet so failure leaves system clean.

sub_clause.amendment: Extend dispatch line 96 `$patterns` array from `@('*.log', '*.jsonl', '*.json.bak', 'state.json*', '*.history')` to `@('*.log', '*.jsonl', '*.json.bak', 'state.json*', '*.history', 'lifts.json', 'escalation_seen.json')`. Per F.1 Option A "preserves stragglers; minimal cost". Captures 2 runtime artifacts (`tools/autonomy_budget/lifts.json`, `tools/fleet_observability/escalation_seen.json`) that current pattern set misses; ~2 KB forensic metadata per file at archive time.
sub_clause.citation: audit MD §1 + §20 F.1; Workflow-Integrity-Review S-2; F.1 LA disposition.

sub_clause.amendment: Correct dispatch line 14 from `Test-Path C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\tools\fleet_ops\blarai_next_task_resolver.py` to `Test-Path C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\tools\autonomy_budget\blarai_next_task_resolver.py`. The resolver lives at `autonomy_budget/`, not `fleet_ops/`, per Workflow-Integrity-Review §4 S-1 + AUDIT_RISK_REVIEW §0.6 R12. Stage 5.1 wholesale-deletes both directories so the verification still passes post-5.1, but for the right reason.

REPLACE dispatch lines 187-189 `` block with the 8-glob expansion per aug1: ``` devplatform/tools/fleet_ops/*.py devplatform/tools/fleet_observability/*.py devplatform/tools/autonomy_budget/*.py devplatform/tools/scheduled-tasks/*.ps1 devplatform/tools/scheduled-tasks/*.xml devplatform/tools/vikunja_mcp/**/*.py devplatform/tools/gate_stale_cleaner/*.py devplatform/docs/scheduled/wake_templates/*.md ``` AND amend the description with audit-acceptance criteria: "EA-9 verifies via `Select-String -Pattern 'C:\\Users\\mrbla\\BlarAI(?!-worktrees)\\tools' -Path <each glob>` AND classifies every live-code hit as either (a) preserve target-project (`-BlarAIRoot` defaults), (b) preserve historical log/test fixture, or (c) DRIFT requiring rewrite. Comment-only hits in `#`-prefix lines are auto-classified preserve unless the comment is doctrinally mis-pointing." Note: All 8 globs are already CLEAN of substantive BlarAI/tools refs per audit MD §18 aug1 verification — but the dispatch's literal 3-glob enumeration is incomplete-by-spec; aug1 closes the scope gap.
sub_clause.citation: audit MD §6, §18 aug1; F.4 dual-track posture; aug1 binding.

sub_clause.amendment:
Extend dispatch lines 199-206 `` block to include 3 additional patterns capturing platform-doctrine `.md` files currently missed: ```
C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\DOMAIN*.md
C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\domain*.md
C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\DEC*.md ``` Captures `DOMAIN8_CLARIFICATIONS_v1.md`, `DOMAIN9_KICKOFF_PROMPT.md`, `domain8_spike_findings.md` (lowercase prefix) per audit MD §7. The `DEC*.md` pattern is forward-compatibility (no current matches; symmetric with `DEC*.xml`).

**REPLACE wholesale `Remove-Item -Recurse -Force C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\governance` (dispatch line 30) with SELECTIVE delete preserving 13 BlarAI-internal architectural governance docs per A14 disposition.**
Replacement command for the governance portion of Item 5.5.v2: ```powershell $governanceMigrated = @( 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\governance\STYLE.md', 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\governance\fleet-hygiene.md', 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\governance\merge-policy.md', 'C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\governance\parallel-sprints.md' ) foreach ($p in $governanceMigrated) { if (Test-Path $p) { Remove-Item -Force $p } } ``` KEEP the wake_templates wholesale-delete unchanged (`Remove-Item -Recurse -Force C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\scheduled\wake_templates`) — 6/6 templates were fully migrated at Stage 3.2.v2 per .copy_manifest_v2.yaml.
Update `` line accordingly: `Test-Path C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\governance` → expected True (directory + 13 BlarAI-internal docs retained); `Test-Path C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\scheduled\wake_templates` → expected False.
The 13 BlarAI-internal governance docs preserved by this amendment: README.md (governance index), circuit-breaker.md, configuration-management.md, context-spotlighting.md, deployment-verification.md, error-recovery.md, gpu-runtime.md, ipc-protocol.md, observability.md, pgov-validation.md, rule-engine.md, session-state.md, streaming-output.md.
RATIONALE: the dispatch's description (line 24) claims "Copied to devplatform in Stage 3.2.v2 — now DELETE from BlarAI" — implying 17/17 governance docs were migrated. Reality per A14 + on-disk verification (Glob): only 4/17 migrated (the fleet-relevant subset: STYLE, fleet-hygiene, merge-policy, parallel-sprints). The other 13 are BlarAI-internal architectural governance (USE-CASE policies, ADR templates, hardware/runtime docs) and stay at BlarAI per the explicit Stage 3 selective-copy decision (g7-ea1_n12). Wholesale-delete contradicts that Stage 3 disposition and would cause irreversible data loss.
LOAD-BEARING: if EA-9 cannot reach LA mid-stage at Item 5.5.v2, fall back to selective-delete (preserve the 13). Data-preservation default per "irreversible operations require explicit affirmation."
sub_clause.citation: audit MD §8 (HIGHEST-IMPACT FINDING); STATUS.md A14; Stage 3 close marker (g7-ea1_n12); on-disk Glob verification (Guide-#9 cross-verified).

sub_clause.amendment: Correct dispatch line 42 path-spec from `docs/ea_queue/archive/ — EA per-sprint archives (per-project)` to `scheduled/ea_queue/archive/ — EA per-sprint archives (per-project)`. The actual location is `BlarAI\scheduled\ea_queue\archive\` (top-level `scheduled/`), which Item 5.3 moves to `.platform/scheduled/ea_queue/archive/`. Pure documentation drift; no executable side-effect.

REPLACE dispatch description line 211 ("remove anything referencing tools/vikunja_mcp, tools/fleet_*, tools/autonomy_budget") with explicit enumeration: ``` Edit `[tool.pytest.ini_options]` testpaths array (lines 6-22 of BlarAI/pyproject.toml). REMOVE these 6 testpaths (post-Stage-5.1 deletion of platform tool dirs): - "tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/tests" - "tools/vikunja_mcp/tests" - "tools/autonomy_budget/tests" - "tools/gate_stale_cleaner/tests" - "tools/fleet_observability/tests" - "tools/fleet_ops/tests" PRESERVE these 9 testpaths (BlarAI runtime — required for 5.8.v2 baseline): - "shared/tests" - "services/policy_agent/tests" - "services/assistant_orchestrator/tests" - "services/semantic_router/tests" - "services/ui_gateway/tests" - "services/ui_shell/tests" - "launcher/tests" - "tests/integration" - "tools/tests" Markers (lines 24-27): asyncio + slow; no platform-tool refs; NO marker removal needed. ``` Without this explicit enumeration, EA-9 might leave `tools/gate_stale_cleaner/tests` in testpaths (dispatch description's "tools/fleet_*" wildcard does NOT match `gate_stale_cleaner`) — post-5.1 deletion of the directory, pytest at 5.8.v2 would emit ImportError/collection failure.
sub_clause.citation: audit MD §10, §19 r4; on-disk Read of BlarAI/pyproject.toml (Guide-#9 cross-verified); r4 binding.

sub_clause.amendment:
REPLACE dispatch description line 215 (vague: "Add `.platform/` contents handling. Typically `.platform/` SHOULD be tracked... but secrets/tokens inside should be gitignored") with concrete `.gitignore` additions to make at Item 5.7: ``` # Wake launcher mutex (per fleet-hygiene §1 Drift Catalogue Pattern A; # same drift class as the UAT2 entries that caused 60+ accumulated # wake_launcher auto-stashes in 2026-04-22/23). Per w20. .claude/scheduled_tasks.lock
# Stage 5.0.5 archive output (large binary; forensic-only) .platform/audit_archive/*.zip .platform/audit_archive/pre_stage5_*/ ``` Track .platform/sprints/, .platform/scheduled/, .platform/active_tasks.yaml, .platform/docs_sprints/, .platform/vikunja_project_ids.yaml — these are project state, NOT runtime artifacts. EA-9 verifies post-edit `git status --short` shows the .platform/ contents staged correctly.
sub_clause.citation: audit MD §11; w20 watchpoint; .gitignore on-disk verification (Guide-#9 cross-verified).

sub_clause.amendment:
Mark Item 5.8 (lines 218-221) SUPERSEDED-BY-5.8.v2 in dispatch text. Replace `` line 220 from "Same baseline (755 passed, 2 skipped). Or full scope (835 passed, 2 skipped) if live runtime available" with: "**SUPERSEDED BY 5.8.v2**: ~981 passed, 22 skipped on `pytest shared/ services/ launcher/ --tb=short -q`. The historical 755-baseline reference is pre-Sprint-8 and obsolete per CLAUDE.md §Active State + STATUS.md §v2 Banner."
Operationally: EA-9 runs Item 5.8.v2 command (which includes `launcher/` directory and uses regression scope), NOT the v1 5.8 command. If EA-9 runs v1 5.8 first, sees ~981 passed (not 755), it could mis-interpret as +226-test regression-discovery. False alarm; n1(i) prevents.
sub_clause.citation: audit MD §12; CLAUDE.md §Active State; STATUS.md §v2 Banner line 24.

sub_clause.amendment:
REPLACE Item 5.9 (lines 223-231) entirely with the F.2 30-min mini-soak protocol per LA F.2 disposition. Original dispatch references DISABLED `BlarAI_daily_digest` (Phase A 2026-04-29 disabling); task name is wrong format (should be `Daily Digest` Title Case Spaces, not underscore form).
Replacement Item 5.9: ```xml
F.2 disposition (per LA 2026-04-29): replace original BlarAI_daily_digest probe (DISABLED per Phase A) with a 30-min observational soak across 2 full 15-min wake-* cycles post-Item-5.7 commit. Verify all 4 wake-* roles + cadence_monitor (every 5 min) + escalation_watchdog (every 5 min) produce expected silent-success outputs. **Mature-not-minimal upgrade per Phase D soak methodology proportional to Stage 5.**
Write-Host "Starting 30-min mini-soak from $((Get-Date).ToString('HH:mm:ss'))..." $start = Get-Date $deadline = $start.AddMinutes(30) while ((Get-Date) -lt $deadline) { Start-Sleep 60 $elapsed = ((Get-Date) - $start).TotalMinutes Write-Host "Elapsed $([math]::Round($elapsed,1)) min..." } Write-Host "`n=== Post-soak fleet state ===" Get-ScheduledTask -TaskPath '\BlarAI\' | Get-ScheduledTaskInfo | Where-Object { $_.LastRunTime -ge $start } | Select TaskName, LastRunTime, LastTaskResult | Format-Table -AutoSize
During the 30-min window, expected firings (cadence × 30 min): - Wake SDO, Wake Co-Lead Architect, Wake EA Code (every 15 min, 2 firings each = 6 total) - Sprint Auditor (every 15 min, 2 firings) - Agents Cadence Monitor (every 5 min, 6 firings) - Escalation Watchdog (every 5 min, 6 firings) - Credentials Rotation Reminder (typically daily; likely won't fire in 30-min window — NOT a failure if absent) - Gate Stale Cleaner (typically hourly; may not fire in 30-min window — NOT a failure if absent) ACCEPTANCE: all tasks that DO fire within the 30-min window show LastTaskResult=0. Tasks that don't fire due to longer cadence (e.g., Credentials Rotation Reminder daily, Gate Stale Cleaner hourly) are NOT a failure condition — only firings are evaluated. Wake-* tasks: WORK-GATE skip + NO-WORK-EXIT (verify via devplatform/tools/scheduled-tasks/logs/<role>.log entries; NO claude.exe child processes). Cadence Monitor + Escalation Watchdog: silent-success markers per Phase D evidence pattern.
``` Per F.2 binding decision; per LA mature-not-minimal motto.
sub_clause.citation: audit MD §14, §20 F.2; F.2 LA disposition (UPGRADED).

sub_clause.amendment: Add precondition guard before Item 5.11 tag commands: ```powershell $onMain = (git -C C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI branch --show-current) -eq 'main' if (-not $onMain) { Write-Warning "Item 5.11 expected to run AFTER 5.10 merge to main. Current branch: $((git -C C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI branch --show-current)). Tagging chore/platform-extraction tip; LA may need to re-tag on main post-merge." } git -C C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI tag post-platform-extract -m "BlarAI cleanup complete, devplatform authoritative" git -C C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform tag platform-extracted -m "devplatform established from BlarAI extraction" ``` Cosmetic only; if LA REJECTS at 5.10 HALT, tags created on chore/platform-extraction tip are still meaningful (close-state marker). If LA APPROVES (typical path), tags land on main via the merge commit. PRESERVE existing `pre-platform-extract` tag (BlarAI side; per RECOVERY.md asset coverage).
sub_clause.citation: audit MD §16; w10 watchpoint (Item 5.10 LA-merge gate).

sub_clause.amendment:
INSERT new "Step 0 - Rollback Readiness Verification" in EA-9 init pointer Pre-Flight section, BEFORE Step 1 (state.pause_fleet on devplatform). Embed the consolidated PowerShell block from Guide-#8 dispatch §check_definition → <consolidated_powershell_block> verbatim (5 checks; ~30s runtime).
The 5 critical checks: 1. `pre-platform-extract` git tag exists on BlarAI (rollback target) 2. Bundle file exists at `C:\Users\mrbla\backups\BlarAI_pre_extract.bundle` 3. OOG (out-of-git) zip exists at `C:\Users\mrbla\backups\blarai_oog_20260424_171721.zip` 4. Bundle SHA256 matches `C051CCFB8FBC6CA31E03F48537816F56CC3FAFC927CEAFAEC9825C9EC65D7707` (Stage 0 manifest) 5. devplatform git log includes Phase C wake_launcher fix at `be22a15`
Step 0 placement BEFORE Step 1 (fleet pause) is intentional: read-only check; failure leaves system clean (no fleet pause to revert, no branch checkout to undo). PASS authorizes EA-9 to proceed to Step 1 (existing fleet-pause SOP per `.github/copilot-instructions.md §fleet_pause_sop`).
Failure handling: any single FAIL → HARD STOP. EA-9 reports to LA via chat with check ID + failure detail + per-check failure_disposition guidance (verbatim from Guide-#8 dispatch §per_check_details). EA-9 does NOT autonomously execute recovery (tag recreation, bundle regeneration, OOG-zip restore, Phase C fix re-application) - those are LA decisions because they involve modifying baseline state.
Output capture: EA-9 captures the inline PowerShell output text block in the Stage 5 close STATUS append (Item 5.12 - under a "Pre-Flight Verification Outputs" subsection per Guide-#8 dispatch §success_criteria).
Optional mature-not-minimal extensions if EA-9 wall-clock budget permits (per Guide-#8 dispatch §optional_extensions; +15-25s runtime; same HARD STOP discipline): - ext1: OOG zip SHA256 verification against `15B4B9A138974F500FFAE9682350F333DC228AC94A1DB184E26A220508A129A8` - ext2: 13 scheduled task XML backups exist at `C:\Users\mrbla\backups\scheduled_tasks_export\` - ext3: devplatform branch is `main` (not branched off mid-Stage) LA may direct EA-9 to include or skip these extensions in iter-1 comprehension gate.
sub_clause.citation: Guide-#8 dispatch `5.0-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_8_ROLLBACK_READINESS_PREFLIGHT_FOR_EA_9.xml` (committed at 9e92b26); LA directive 2026-04-29 (rollback-readiness verification chosen instead of 4th audit pass; mature-not-minimal procedural step in lieu of additional audit pass given diminishing returns); STATUS.md Recovery Anchors table; STATUS.md Stage 4 Closure Footnotes §4 (Phase C remediation) + Anomaly A19 (BUG 1 fix at devplatform be22a15).
sub_clause.execution_status_for_this_iter_1_gate: EXECUTED at 2026-04-30T04:25Z. ALL 5 CHECKS PASS (output captured verbatim in §B above). EA-9 proceeded to Step 1 per success-path authorization. Optional ext1-ext3 extensions: NOT executed in this Pre-Flight pass per default-skip; LA may direct to run at iter-1 review if desired (informational; bundle SHA passed already).

section_d.name: Required Acknowledgments (VERBATIM)
section_d.intro: 13 required_acknowledgments per init pointer §required_acknowledgments_for_iter_1_gate + verdict §required_acknowledgments. ack9 (LOAD-BEARING; n1(e) selective-vs-wholesale) AND ack13 (n1(n) Step 0 HARD STOP discipline) are blocking.
section_d.acknowledge: I will restate the cumulative-ack chain VERBATIM (116 atoms inherited: g7-ea1_n10..n13 + g7-ea7_n14 + g8-ea8_n1(a..p) + g8-ea8_n2(a..f) + g8-ea8_n3(a..l)) per dn5/dn22/s9w1.

I will restate g9-ea9_n1 VERBATIM with all 14 sub-clauses (a) through (n), including the verbatim text of n1(e) Class A LOAD-BEARING amendment converting Item 5.5.v2 wholesale-delete to selective 4-of-17 + n1(n) Pre-Flight Step 0 rollback-readiness verification per Guide-#8 dispatch at 9e92b26.

I will pause the fleet via `state.pause_fleet('Stage 5 cleanup', updated_by='ea_code_blarai_helper', path='C:/Users/mrbla/devplatform/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json')` then commit `chore(ops): pause fleet -- Stage 5 cleanup` BEFORE any branch checkout / non-trivial edit, per `.github/copilot-instructions.md §fleet_pause_sop`. The state.json target is **devplatform/** (NOT BlarAI/; BlarAI/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json is a dead artifact post-Phase-C4 per w21+dn31; auto-resolved at Stage 5.1 directory deletion per Ticket KK). [EXECUTED at 2026-04-30T04:27:50Z; devplatform commit 1a552d9.]
I will NOT pop or apply ANY wake_launcher auto-stash entry per dn10 + w8 (corrected per A20). Discipline is generic regardless of timestamp. [V0i confirmed 2 stash entries; both LEFT in place.]

I will NOT delete `BlarAI/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json` directly per dn31 + w21. Stage 5.1 deletes the parent directory which auto-resolves it. [V0h confirmed file present and untouched; M-state from BUG-5 emergency pause carries forward.]
I will NOT execute Stage 5.1 deletions before Item 5.0.5 audit archive completes per dn32. Item ordering: 5.0.5 → 5.1.
I will NOT edit BlarAI's CLAUDE.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, or AGENTS.md per dn3 (V8 / 6.1.v2 deferred).
I will NOT merge `chore/platform-extraction` to `main` until LA explicitly approves per R10 + w10. Item 5.10 has explicit HALT for this; n1(k) extends with review matrix.

**LOAD-BEARING per n1(e)**: I will execute n1(e)'s selective-delete amendment at Item 5.5.v2 (preserving 13 BlarAI-internal governance docs per A14), NOT the original dispatch wholesale-delete. If at execution time the dispatch text appears unamended, I STOP and verify with LA before proceeding. Data-preservation default if LA unreachable: selective-delete.

I will spot-check Item 5.12 STATUS.md append for duplication BEFORE committing per d9 + A17. Use `Add-Content -NoNewline` or hash-verified append helper if available; otherwise post-write hash-verification.

Guide-#9 will spawn 3 parallel verification subagents at Stage 5 close (per d6 + w14). I cooperate by providing requested artifacts (file lists, git diff outputs) but do NOT manage subagent design. Independent disk-level verification by Guide-#9 happens AFTER subagents emit; my role at close is comprehensive close-report cross-checked by Guide-#9 per w11/dn14.

Cross-check discipline: my "I did X" reports must be backed by independent `git diff` / `Test-Path` / `Get-ChildItem` evidence. EA-paraphrase-introduced-error pattern (w11) is a Guide-#9 cross-verification standard; close-report under-enumeration triggers re-verify per dn14/dn20/dn24.

I will run Step 0 (rollback-readiness verification) BEFORE state.pause_fleet. If any of the 5 checks fail, I HARD STOP and report to LA with the failure_disposition guidance from the dispatch. I do NOT autonomously execute recovery (tag recreation, bundle regeneration, etc.) - those are LA decisions. [EXECUTED at 2026-04-30T04:25Z; ALL 5 CHECKS PASS; EA-9 proceeded to Step 1 per success path.]

la_pre_prep_attestation_recap.preamble: Per init pointer §la_pre_prep_checklist, EA-9's iter-1 gate confirms each LA attestation status. Stage 5 only requires prep-1 + prep-4 (prep-2 + prep-3 are N/A).
la_pre_prep_attestation_recap.prep: EA-9 did not observe the explicit attestation line `[ATTESTATION prep-1] Other AI surfaces closed...` in the LA spawn message. The user's prose stated "LA pre-prep attestations: provided in this message above the chat-paste line", but the explicit attestation line(s) are not visible to EA-9. EA-9 requests Guide-#9 confirm at PROCEED whether prep-1 attestation is satisfied (LA closed Claude Desktop + other VS Code workspaces + Claude Code terminals before EA-9 spawn). Note: Stage 5 has no MCP-server kill at Pre-Flight (unlike Stage 4 Item 4.0 narrowed kill); the prep-1 risk is lower for Stage 5, but the attestation discipline applies.
N/A — Stage 5 does not require VIKUNJA_TOKEN (no Vikunja REST work; F.2 mini-soak observes via `Get-ScheduledTaskInfo`, not Vikunja queries).
N/A — Stage 5 does not require elevated PowerShell (no S4U scheduled-task work; gitignore add for `.claude/scheduled_tasks.lock` runs in non-elevated terminal).

EA-9 did not observe the explicit `[ATTESTATION prep-4] Available for ~3-4 hour block...` line. EA-9 requests Guide-#9 confirm LA availability for the full ~3-4h block including the 30-min F.2 mini-soak and reachability for Item 5.5.v2 selective-delete confirmation gate (per ack9) AND Item 5.10 pre-merge review matrix approval. If LA is NOT reachable mid-stage at Item 5.5.v2, EA-9 falls back to selective-delete per n1(e) data-preservation default.

section_e.name: Work Items + Files Touched + Verification Strategy
section_e.intro: 16 Stage 5 items (12 v1 + 4 v2) per `06_STAGE5_CLEANUP.xml`, plus Pre-Flight Step 0 (NEW per n1(n)) + Step 1 (state.pause_fleet) + Stage-5-close unpause. Each row maps dispatch-instruction → g9-ea9_n1 amendment (if any) → files-touched → verification → STOP-and-report trigger.

item.files_touched: none (read-only)
item.verification: 5 checks: tag exists, bundle exists, OOG zip exists, bundle SHA matches, Phase C fix at be22a15 in devplatform git log. ALL PASS.
item.stop_trigger: t1 (LOAD-BEARING): any FAIL → HARD STOP, report failure_disposition guidance from Guide-#8 dispatch. NOT TRIGGERED.

item.files_touched: devplatform/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json (modified to fleet_paused=true) + devplatform git tree (new commit 1a552d9 on main)
item.verification: state.json paused=True, reason='Stage 5 cleanup', updated_by='ea_code_blarai_helper'; commit 1a552d9 ahead of 45aec52.
item.stop_trigger: none specified; Step 1 is straightforward fleet-pause SOP application.

item.files_touched: none (read-only)
item.verification: V0a..V0n checked; 2 STATUS-staleness drift items documented in §B v0_drift_summary; 0 procedure-breakage drift items.

item.files_touched: creates `BlarAI/.platform/audit_archive/pre_stage5_.zip` from 6 about-to-be-deleted dirs (tools/fleet_ops, tools/fleet_observability, tools/autonomy_budget, tools/gate_stale_cleaner, tools/scheduled-tasks, tools/vikunja_mcp); patterns include `*.log`, `*.jsonl`, `*.json.bak`, `state.json*`, `*.history`, `lifts.json`, `escalation_seen.json` (n1(a) extension).
item.verification: Test-Path on archive zip; verify `$copied -gt 0` post-archive; if `$copied -eq 0` AND source dirs non-empty → t3 trigger.
item.stop_trigger: t3 (HIGH): pattern miss / no files captured → STOP before 5.1.

item.files_touched: delete 9 paths: tools/vikunja, tools/vikunja_mcp, tools/fleet_ops, tools/fleet_observability, tools/autonomy_budget, tools/gate_stale_cleaner, tools/scheduled-tasks, tools/_vikunja_client.py, tools/_project_context.py. PRESERVE tools/openvino_contrib_agent + tools/tests.
item.verification: Foreach Test-Path returns False for all 9 paths post-delete; `Test-Path tools/openvino_contrib_agent` = True; `Test-Path tools/tests` = True. Optional Class D cleanup of orphan `tools/__pycache__/` post-deletion.
item.stop_trigger: any unexpected residual file beyond cache orphans → investigate.

item.files_touched: none (verify-only step; n1(b) corrects path for `blarai_next_task_resolver.py` from `fleet_ops/` to `autonomy_budget/`).
item.verification: 5 Test-Path checks (all expected False post-5.1).

item.files_touched: BlarAI/.platform/ directory (already exists from Stage 1.5 with vikunja_project_ids.yaml). `New-Item -Force` is idempotent.
item.verification: Test-Path returns True.

item.files_touched: 4 moves: BlarAI/sprints/ → .platform/sprints/, BlarAI/scheduled/ → .platform/scheduled/, BlarAI/docs/active_tasks.yaml → .platform/active_tasks.yaml, BlarAI/docs/sprints/ → .platform/docs_sprints/. Wrapped in try/catch with `$applied` rollback per n1(m).
item.verification: Get-ChildItem .platform -Recurse -Depth 1; 4 destinations populated; original 4 source paths return False on Test-Path.
item.stop_trigger: t4 (HIGH): mid-loop Move-Item failure → try/catch rolls back applied moves; report to LA which moves succeeded vs failed.

item.files_touched: read-only across 8 globs in devplatform: fleet_ops/*.py, fleet_observability/*.py, autonomy_budget/*.py, scheduled-tasks/*.ps1, scheduled-tasks/*.xml, vikunja_mcp/**/*.py, gate_stale_cleaner/*.py, docs/scheduled/wake_templates/*.md.
item.verification: Select-String pattern `'C:\\Users\\mrbla\\BlarAI(?!-worktrees)\\tools'` per glob; classify each hit as preserve (target-project / log/test fixture / forensic / comment-only) vs DRIFT-rewrite. Findings flow into 5.10 HALT-gate matrix [1].
item.stop_trigger: t7 (MED): unexpected DRIFT requiring rewrite → record in matrix; do not auto-fix per t11 (Phase-C-pattern resurfacing → bind under g9-ea9_n*).

item.files_touched: delete from BlarAI/docs/: CLAUDE_*.md, CLAUDE_*.xml, CO_LEAD_*.xml, DEC*.xml, DOMAIN*.xml, DOMAIN*.md (n1(d) extension), domain*.md (n1(d) extension lowercase), DEC*.md (n1(d) forward-compat), CONTINUATION_PROMPT.md. Powershell Remove-Item non-recursive default per dn13 — temp_for_responses/ is safe (verified r3 NOT-REAL by Guide-#9).
item.verification: Get-ChildItem with same patterns post-delete returns empty.
item.stop_trigger: t5 (HIGH): pre-deletion dry-run shows ANY pattern matches `docs\platform_separation\temp_for_responses\` → HARD STOP (would destroy Guide audit chain).

item.files_touched: SELECTIVE delete from BlarAI/docs/governance/: STYLE.md, fleet-hygiene.md, merge-policy.md, parallel-sprints.md (4 of 17). PRESERVE 13 BlarAI-internal docs (README, circuit-breaker, configuration-management, context-spotlighting, deployment-verification, error-recovery, gpu-runtime, ipc-protocol, observability, pgov-validation, rule-engine, session-state, streaming-output). Wholesale delete BlarAI/docs/scheduled/wake_templates/ (6/6 fully migrated at Stage 3.2.v2).
item.verification: `Test-Path docs/governance` = True (dir + 13 files retained); `Test-Path docs/scheduled/wake_templates` = False; `Get-ChildItem docs/governance | Measure-Object` = 13 files; the 4 deleted docs absent.
item.stop_trigger: t2 (LOAD-BEARING): if dispatch text appears unamended (wholesale `Remove-Item -Recurse -Force docs\governance`) → STOP, verify with LA. Data-preservation default if LA unreachable: selective-delete (per ack9 + n1(e)).

item.files_touched: none (documentation amendment per dispatch line 42; corrects path-spec `docs/ea_queue/archive/` → `scheduled/ea_queue/archive/`).
item.verification: cosmetic; no executable side-effect.

item.files_touched: BlarAI/pyproject.toml [tool.pytest.ini_options] testpaths array (lines 6-22). REMOVE 6 platform-tool testpaths; PRESERVE 9 BlarAI runtime testpaths.
item.verification: post-edit Read of pyproject.toml shows exact 9 testpaths; markers (asyncio + slow) unchanged.

item.files_touched: BlarAI/.gitignore — add 3 lines: `.claude/scheduled_tasks.lock`, `.platform/audit_archive/*.zip`, `.platform/audit_archive/pre_stage5_*/`.
item.verification: post-edit `git status --short` shows `.claude/scheduled_tasks.lock` no longer untracked; `.platform/sprints/`, `.platform/scheduled/`, `.platform/active_tasks.yaml`, `.platform/docs_sprints/`, `.platform/vikunja_project_ids.yaml` STAGED (project state, tracked).

item.files_touched: none (marked SUPERSEDED-BY-5.8.v2; EA-9 runs 5.8.v2 directly).
read-only pytest invocation: `cd BlarAI; .\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1; pytest shared/ services/ launcher/ --tb=short -q`.
item.verification: Expected ~981 passed, 22 skipped (regression scope). Allow ±5 fluctuation per t6.
item.stop_trigger: t6 (HIGH): baseline regression below ~981 passed / 22 skipped (±20+ delta) → report failures; LA decides revert-or-fix.

item.files_touched: read-only soak observation; Get-ScheduledTaskInfo polling. NO state mutation.
item.verification: 30-min real-time soak post-Item-5.7 commit. ACCEPTANCE: firing tasks LastTaskResult=0; non-firing NOT failure (Credentials Rotation Reminder daily, Gate Stale Cleaner hourly may not fire). Wake-* WORK-GATE skip + NO-WORK-EXIT (NO claude.exe child processes per fleet-paused state). Cadence Monitor + Escalation Watchdog silent-success markers per Phase D evidence pattern.

item.files_touched: BlarAI git tree (new commit on chore/platform-extraction with all 5.0.5-5.9 changes); does NOT merge to main without LA approval.
item.verification: Print 4-row HALT-gate matrix per n1(k) aug2 with actual PASS/FAIL values: [1] 5.4 audit (8 globs PASS/FAIL), [2] 5.8.v2 baseline (~981/22 PASS/FAIL), [3] 5.9 mini-soak (firing-task LastTaskResult=0 PASS/FAIL; non-firing NOT failure), [4] DIFF SUMMARY. EA-9 fills actual values, NOT placeholders. LA approves merge ONLY when [1][2][3] all show PASS.
item.stop_trigger: t9 (MED): EA-9 attempts merge to main outside Item 5.10 LA-approval gate → HARD STOP per ack8/R10/w10.

item.files_touched: BlarAI tag `post-platform-extract`, devplatform tag `platform-extracted`; precondition guard checks `git branch --show-current` is `main` post-5.10 merge (warn-not-throw if still on chore/platform-extraction).
item.verification: `git tag -l "post-platform-extract"` returns the tag name on BlarAI; `git tag -l "platform-extracted"` on devplatform.

item.files_touched: STATUS.md — append Stage 5 Closure Footnotes (Stage 3/4 closure pattern). Hash-verify pre-vs-post per d9/A17/EX-4.
item.verification: SHA256 hash pre-append vs post-append shows single-line append-zone; no duplication. EA-9 close report items per init pointer §ea_9_close_report_requirements.
item.stop_trigger: EX-4 — hash-mismatch indicates duplication; investigate before commit.

item.files_touched: devplatform/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json (resume_fleet); devplatform git tree (new unpause commit).
item.verification: state.json paused=False; commit `chore(ops): unpause fleet -- Stage 5 cleanup done` on devplatform main.

section_f.name: Risks / Ambiguities / Open Questions
section_f.risk: Item 5.5.v2 is the highest-stakes step in Stage 5: the dispatch text says wholesale `Remove-Item -Recurse -Force docs\governance` (line 30); the audit verdict (n1(e)) and EA-9 init pointer convert this to selective 4-of-17 preserving 13 BlarAI-internal docs. Guide-#9 / LA must confirm at PROCEED that EA-9 applies the n1(e) selective amendment, NOT the dispatch literal. Per ack9 / data-preservation default, if LA unreachable mid-stage, EA-9 falls back to selective-delete.

EA-9 did not observe explicit `[ATTESTATION prep-1]` or `[ATTESTATION prep-4]` lines in the LA spawn message. The user prose stated "LA pre-prep attestations: provided in this message above the chat-paste line" but those lines are not present. Stage 5's prep-1 risk is lower than Stage 4 (no MCP-server kill at Pre-Flight); however the discipline applies. EA-9 requests Guide-#9 confirm at PROCEED whether prep-1 + prep-4 are satisfied. LA reachability for Item 5.5.v2 confirmation gate (per ack9) and Item 5.10 pre-merge review matrix (per ack8/n1(k)) is contingent on prep-4.

Two doc-defects in init pointer's V0 pickup expected text (V0a BlarAI HEAD = 176922c → actual ff39a41; V0c BlarAI WT clean post-Step-1 → actual M state.json). Both consistent with §step_1 canonical "devplatform-only commit" + §identity hard-invariant "EA-9 does NOT touch BlarAI's state.json directly". EA-9 classified as Operating Rule 5b(a) STATUS staleness (document and proceed). Requesting Guide-#9 confirm classification at PROCEED. Alternative interpretation: if Guide-#9 intends BlarAI side ALSO receive a pause commit (capturing BUG-5 dead-artifact M state for clean diff at 5.1), please direct in PROCEED message; EA-9 will execute additional commit on BlarAI side.

Per audit MD §24, 5 items may surface during Stage 5 execution: (1) PowerShell `Remove-Item -Recurse -Force` filesystem-lock if wake_launcher firing concurrent (mitigated by Step-1 pause); (2) BlarAI/.mcp.json literal-password Phase C revert is gitignored per Ticket I, so 5.10 `git add -A` doesn't include it (verified safe); (3) STATUS.md duplication recurrence per d9/A17 — EA-9 hash-verifies pre-vs-post per ack10/EX-4; (4) test baseline drift between audit and execution (allow ±5 per t6); (5) devplatform unpause commit at audit time → EA-9's Step-1 pause now sets fleet_paused=true.

EA-9 must back every "I did X" report with `git diff` / `Test-Path` / `Get-ChildItem` evidence. Per ack12 + w11 EA-paraphrase-introduced-error pattern, Guide-#9 cross-verifies on disk. EA-9 commits to providing artifacts on Guide-#9 request without managing subagent design.

If Item 5.4 audit surfaces another PS1 script using `$RepoRoot` for state/log paths instead of `$PSScriptRoot` derivation (Phase C BUG 1/4 pattern), EA-9 binds as new finding under `g9-ea9_n*`; does NOT silently fix per dn35.
section_f.ambiguity: EA-9 ran the 5 critical Step 0 checks; did NOT run optional ext1 (OOG zip SHA verification), ext2 (13 scheduled task XML backups exist), ext3 (devplatform branch is main). The init pointer says "LA may direct EA-9 to include or skip these extensions in iter-1 comprehension gate". EA-9 default-skipped (5/5 critical PASS suffices for rollback parachute confirmation). Guide-#9 may direct re-run with extensions at PROCEED if mature-not-minimal posture warrants.

ambiguity.related_to: working directory for python helper invocation

section_f.ambiguity: The init pointer §step_1 says "Working directory for `python -c` invocation: `C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform`". EA-9 used `Push-Location` to enter devplatform before invoking `python -`, then `Pop-Location` to return. The state.pause_fleet `path=` argument explicitly specifies devplatform's state.json regardless of import source, but using devplatform's authoritative state.py (vs BlarAI's dead-artifact state.py) is procedurally cleaner. EA-9 used the safer pattern; verifies via post-pause state.json read.
section_f.open_question: Per init pointer V0 pickup field BlarAI_HEAD drift handling: "if HEAD is at 176922c (no advance), Step 1 didn't commit; investigate" — this implies a BlarAI commit was expected. EA-9's interpretation is the field has a doc-defect (per §step_1 canonical only commits on devplatform); EA-9 documents and proceeds per 5b(a). Confirming with Guide-#9 at PROCEED.

section_g.name: Stage 5 Close Protocol Acknowledgment
section_g.triple_subagent_verification_at_item_5_1:
EA-9 acknowledges per ack11 + d6 + w14: at Stage 5 close (Item 5.12), Guide-#9 spawns 3 parallel verification subagents: - **S1 (BlarAI-side technical correctness)**: 9 platform tool dirs deleted; .platform/ populated correctly (sprints/, scheduled/, active_tasks.yaml, docs_sprints/, audit_archive/, vikunja_project_ids.yaml); BlarAI/docs/ platform-doctrine docs deleted per 5.5+5.5.v2; .gitignore augmented incl `.claude/scheduled_tasks.lock`; pyproject.toml updated; baseline preserved (~981/22). - **S2 (devplatform-side path-reference correctness)**: 5.4 audit findings applied; 8-glob audit surfaces clean of BlarAI/tools/ refs (or correctly preserved as target-project / comment / fixture / forensic); fleet executes successfully post-Stage-5. - **S3 (procedural completeness)**: all 12+4 v2 sub-items + Pre-Flight Step 0 + Step 1 executed; 8 exit_criteria met; audit archive intact for forensic recovery; tags created (post-platform-extract on BlarAI + platform-extracted on devplatform); STATUS.md updated; merge HALT-gate respected.
EA-9 cooperates by providing requested artifacts (file lists, git diff outputs, Test-Path results); does NOT manage subagent design. Guide-#9 cross-verifies subagent claims on disk per dn14/dn20/dn24/A18.
section_g.ea_9_close_report_requirements: Per init pointer §ea_9_close_report_requirements, EA-9's close report at Item 5.12 includes: - Pre-Flight Step 0 PASS/FAIL summary + check details (verbatim from Step 0 PowerShell output) — already captured in §B. - Pre-Flight Step 1 commit SHA — `1a552d9` on devplatform main. - V0 pickup verification results (any drift documented per Operating Rule 5b) — already in §B. - Per-item completion summary (16 items + Pre-Flight) with outcome + any anomalies. - F.2 30-min mini-soak observations (firing-task LastTaskResult=0 + non-firing tasks documented as not-failure). - 5.10 HALT-gate review matrix (4 rows; actual PASS/FAIL values; not placeholders). - LA approval timestamp (when LA approved merge per w10). - Merge commit SHA (BlarAI side post-LA-approval). - Tag SHAs (post-platform-extract BlarAI + platform-extracted devplatform). - STATUS.md append diff stats (verify single-copy invariant per d9/A17/EX-4). - Stage 5 close unpause commit SHA (devplatform side; LAST action before exit).
section_g.ea_9_self_discipline: EA-9 commits to: - Per w11 / dn14: every "I did X" report cross-verifiable against `git diff` / `Test-Path` / `Get-ChildItem` evidence. - Per ack10: hash-verify Item 5.12 STATUS append pre-vs-post BEFORE committing. - Per ack8 + w10: NEVER merge to main without LA explicit approval. - Per dn3: NO edits to BlarAI's CLAUDE.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, AGENTS.md. - Per dn10/w8/A20: NO pop or apply of any wake_launcher auto-stash entry. - Per dn31/w21: NO direct touch of BlarAI's state.json (auto-resolved at 5.1). - Per dn32/ack6: NO 5.1 deletion before 5.0.5 audit archive completes. - Per t11/dn35: bind any newly-discovered Phase-C-pattern resurfacing as new finding under g9-ea9_n*; do NOT silently fix.

comprehension_gate.stop_and_wait:
EA-9 STOPS HERE. All Pre-Flight Steps 0-2 executed cleanly. iter-1 comprehension gate emitted. Awaiting Guide-#9 review and PROCEED authorization. Estimated EA-9 iter-1 turn count to PROCEED: 1-2.
PROCEED THRESHOLD per verdict §proceed_authorization: all 13 acknowledgments present (ACK1-ACK13 above) + 14 g9-ea9_n1 sub-clauses restated verbatim (§C above) + 116-atom inherited chain restated verbatim (§C above). Class A n1(e) acknowledgment (ack9, LOAD-BEARING) AND Class C n1(n) acknowledgment (ack13, EXECUTED) BOTH present and unambiguous.
Specific items requested in Guide-#9 PROCEED message: 1. Confirm V0 drift classification (Operating Rule 5b(a) STATUS-staleness per §B v0_drift_summary) is correct interpretation. Alternative (BlarAI-side commit also expected) requires explicit direction. 2. Confirm prep-1 + prep-4 LA attestation status (request from §D la_pre_prep_attestation_recap). 3. (Optional) Direct EA-9 to run ext1-ext3 Step 0 extensions if mature-not-minimal posture warrants (per §F A1). 4. PROCEED authorization for Item 5.0.5 (audit archive) → 5.1 (platform tool dir deletion) execution.
